note: Universal reference thresholds -- verify against applicable country standard. Country modules provide jurisdiction-specific values.

warrants.description: Minimum traffic volumes on major and minor approaches for 8 or more hours on an average day
warrants.notes: Reduced thresholds (70%) apply where population < 10,000 or 85th percentile speed > 70 km/h

warrants.description: Interruption of continuous traffic -- minor road suffers excessive delay for any 4 hours
warrants.notes: Applied when major road volume prevents minor road entry

warrants.description: Peak hour delay on minor road unacceptable
warrants.notes: Only requires 1 hour. Higher thresholds than 8-hour warrant.

warrants.description: Pedestrian crossing demand warrants signal control

thresholds.gap_availability: Fewer than 60 adequate gaps per hour in the traffic stream

warrants.notes: Also applicable if raised median >= 1.5 m width with 1000+ pcu/hr on major road. Reduced thresholds (70%) apply where 85th percentile speed > 60 km/h or population < 150,000.

warrants.description: Signal justified by crash frequency and severity

thresholds.crash_type: injury and/or property damage crashes susceptible to correction by traffic signal
thresholds.condition: Alternatives (signs, markings, channelisation) tried and not effective

warrants.notes: Signal must be likely to reduce crash type. Not for rear-end crashes which may increase with signals.

warrants.description: Gaps in traffic inadequate for school children to cross

thresholds.description: Applied when the number of adequate gaps during school crossing periods is insufficient for safe crossing by school children
thresholds.gap_seconds: Depends on crossing width and walking speed (typically requires longer gaps than adult pedestrians)

warrants.notes: Consider school crossing guards, raised crossings, or other measures before signals

warrants.description: Two or more warrants met at 80% of their criteria
warrants.notes: Engineering judgment required. Trial of other remedial measures should be attempted first.
